Class AppDatabase

java.lang.Object
com.example.codebase.AppDatabase
public class AppDatabase extends Object
Singleton database management class for Firebase Firestore. Provides a central point of access for all Firestore collection references used throughout the app. All activities and fragments should access Firestore through this class rather than calling FirebaseFirestore.getInstance() directly, ensuring consistent collection naming and a single shared connection.

    Description

    Reference to the notifications Firestore collection.
    final com.google.firebase.firestore.CollectionReference
    usersRef
    Reference to the users Firestore collection.

  • Field Details

    • usersRef

      public final com.google.firebase.firestore.CollectionReference usersRef
      Reference to the users Firestore collection. Each document is keyed by the device ID of the user.

    • eventsRef

      public final com.google.firebase.firestore.CollectionReference eventsRef
      Reference to the events Firestore collection. Each document represents a single event created by an organizer.

    • notificationsRef

      public final com.google.firebase.firestore.CollectionReference notificationsRef
      Reference to the notifications Firestore collection. Structured as notifications/{deviceId}/messages/{autoId}. Each sub-document represents a notification sent to an entrant.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static AppDatabase getInstance()
      Returns the singleton instance of AppDatabase. Creates a new instance if one does not already exist. Thread-safe via synchronization.
      Returns:
      the shared AppDatabase instance
